data.title: South Korea increases crypto seizures, vows to target cold wallets in crackdown on tax evasion with home searches.
data.description: The National Tax Service of South Korea has issued a warning that cold wallets are not beyond its jurisdiction. They have announced their intention to carry out home searches in order to tackle tax evasion.

data.title: CIB Marine Bancshares, Inc. reports improved Q3 2025 results with higher net interest income and strong mortgage operations.
data.description: On October 10, 2025, in Brookfield, Wisconsin, CIB Marine Bancshares, Inc. disclosed its unaudited financial results for the quarter and nine months ending on September 30, 2025. The company, which owns CIBM Bank, reported enhanced net interest income and more robust mortgage operations leading to improved overall performance in the third quarter. These results were superior to both the previous quarter and the corresponding period in the preceding year. The company trades under the symbol OTCQX: CIBH.

data.title: Survey shows 1 in 8 managers find Gen Z unemployable, turning to AI. 1,000 U.S. managers interviewed.
data.description: A recent survey conducted by ResumeTemplates.com of 1,000 hiring managers in the U.S. has found that there is a perception among these hiring managers that artificial intelligence is more reliable than Gen Z workers in the workplace. The survey, which was conducted in Seattle on October 10, 2025, highlights a noticeable shift in attitudes towards hiring practices, with concerns growing about the performance and reliability of Generation Z employees. This trend indicates a growing preference for AI technology over young, entry-level workers among employers.

data.title: CRFB criticizes $1.8 trillion deficit, warns of losing superpower status amid 'pointless' shutdown. Borrowing to reach $2 trillion annually.
data.description: A nonpartisan budget watchdog, The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget (CRFB), criticized the ongoing U.S. government shutdown as needless and extravagant, while revealing a massive federal deficit of $1.8 trillion for the fiscal year 2025. The alarming fiscal shortfall was disclosed in the Congressional Budget Office's (CBO) Monthly Budget Review for September, underscoring worries about the nation's fiscal path amid the current political deadlock. CRFB President Maya MacGuineas, in a statement released on Wednesday, expressed apprehension regarding the country's estimated borrowing, emphasizing its unsustainable level. The U.S. is anticipated to borrow nearly $2 trillion annually in the coming decade, prompting questions about the viability of such expansive borrowing.

data.title: Infinitus and Outshift by Cisco partner to improve AI agent interoperability in healthcare, with Infinitus powering over 100 million minutes of conversations.
data.description: Today, in San Francisco, on October 10, 2025, Infinitus Systems, a top agentic communications partner in healthcare, revealed a new partnership with Outshift by Cisco. Infinitus Systems is known for facilitating over 100 million minutes of clinical and administrative conversations. This collaboration aims to streamline processes and improve efficiency within the healthcare industry.

data.title: Stock futures rise after dip on Thursday, with Delta Air Lines (DAL) up 4.3% and PepsiCo (PEP) up 4.2%. Treasury bond yields at 4.11% and 3.58%. 94.6% chances of Fed rate cut in October. Applied Digital (APLD) up 25.74% with $64.2 million revenue, USANA Health Sciences (USNA) down 13% with adjusted loss of 15 cents/share and sales of $214 million in third quarter.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rebounded on Friday after a previous day's decline, with major benchmark indices showing positive movement. Delta Air Lines Inc. (NYSE: DAL) saw a 4.3% increase after beating estimates, while PepsiCo Inc. (NASDAQ: PEP) gained 4.2% after posting an adjusted EPS of $2.29. President Trump hinted at upcoming trade discussions with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.11% and the two-year bond was at 3.58%. The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicated a 94.6% likelihood of a Fed rate cut in October. Futures changes were: Dow Jones 0.10%, S&P 500 0.10%, Nasdaq 100 0.11%, Russell 2000 0.37%. Applied Digital Corp. (NASDAQ: APLD) surged 25.74% in premarket trading after reporting $64.2 million revenue for Q1. USANA Health Sciences Inc. (NYSE: USNA) dropped 13% with a loss of 15 cents per share and $214.00 million in sales for Q3. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) rose 0.11% to $671.93, and the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (NASDAQ: QQQ) advanced 0.14% to $611.53.

data.title: Realsee presents 3D reconstruction & surveying solution at INTERGEO 2025 in Frankfurt, Germany Oct 7-9. #technology #LiDAR
data.description: A company specializing in integrated digital space solutions, Realsee, introduced its advanced 3D scanning solutions using SLAM and LiDAR technology at INTERGEO 2025 in Frankfurt, Germany. The event took place from October 7 to 9 and featured demonstrations of Realsee's capabilities in transitioning from construction sites to property sites. The showcase highlighted the company's innovative approach towards enhancing spatial data collection and analysis.

data.title: Yemeni actor Hamza praised for his perspective on "Rural China" in Shanghai by CNS; offers unique insight.
data.description: On October 10, 2025, Beijing saw the China News Service (CNS) release a statement promoting a Yemeni actor's take on "Rural China" in Shanghai. This actor, named Hamza, shared his unique perspective after experiencing a contrasting side of Shanghai, China's largest metropolis. The actor's insights were discussed in the press release unveiled in Beijing.
Announcement under French Commercial Code L.233-8-II & AMF General Regulation 223-16.

data.title: Crypto sector wary of Bank of England stablecoin cap exemptions as it may hinder growth in the UK's industry.
data.description: Industry representatives have stated that implementing a cap would pose challenges in enforcement and potentially hinder the growth of the United Kingdom's thriving crypto industry.

data.title: In 2024, U.S. secured $2.13B in UN contracts, more than dues paid to UN, accounting for 1 in 10 contracts globally.
data.description: On October 10, 2025, it was reported that the United States maintained its position as the leading provider to UN agencies in 2024, receiving contracts worth $2.13 billion. This amount surpassed the U.S. dues to the UN and constituted approximately 10% of all UN contracts globally. This demonstrates the consistent and significant role of the U.S. as a key supplier to the United Nations.

data.title: Sen. Cynthia Lummis supports Jack Dorsey's call for a tax exemption on Bitcoin transactions, her bill exempts gains up to $300.
data.description: Senator Cynthia Lummis, a Republican from Wyoming, has expressed support for Block Inc. CEO Jack Dorsey's proposal for tax relief on Bitcoin (BTC) transactions. Lummis referenced a bill she previously introduced, known as the 'De Minimis Tax Exemption.' This bill advocates for an exemption on small BTC payments for everyday expenses, such as buying coffee, to prevent triggering capital gains taxes. Lummis highlighted Dorsey's call for this exemption in a tweet, suggesting that her bill already addresses these concerns. The bill aims to exempt gains from small transactions, allowing for up to $300 per transaction and a yearly limit of $5,000 per individual. The legislation has undergone two readings, and more details can be found on Benzinga.com.
